name: Hadoop 2.x (YARN) Architecture
chatResponse: I will break down the Hadoop 2.x architecture, highlighting the decoupling of storage (HDFS) and resource management (YARN).

steps.title: The Shift to YARN
steps.content: Unlike Hadoop 1.x, which combined resource management and data processing in MapReduce, Hadoop 2.x introduced **YARN (Yet Another Resource Negotiator)**. This allows multiple data processing engines (MapReduce, Spark, Tez) to run on a single cluster by treating the cluster's resources as a global pool.

steps.title: HDFS Layer (Storage)
steps.content: The storage layer remains **HDFS**. It consists of a **NameNode** (Master) that manages metadata and file system namespace, and multiple **DataNodes** (Slaves) that store the actual data blocks. Hadoop 2.x also introduced NameNode High Availability (HA) to eliminate the single point of failure.

steps.title: YARN Layer (Resource Management)
steps.content:
YARN functions via two main components:
- **ResourceManager (RM):** The ultimate authority that arbitrates resources among all competing applications.
- **NodeManager (NM):** The per-machine agent responsible for monitoring resource usage (CPU, memory) and reporting to the RM.

steps.title: Application Execution Flow
steps.content:
When a client submits an application:
1. The **ResourceManager** allocates a container to start the **ApplicationMaster (AM)**.
2. The **AM** negotiates resources from the RM and works with **NodeManagers** to execute and monitor tasks within distributed **Containers**.

visualCode:
graph TD
    subgraph Client_Interface
        C[Client Application]
    end

    subgraph YARN_Resource_Management
        RM[ResourceManager]
        S[Scheduler]
        ASM[ApplicationsManager]
        RM --- S
        RM --- ASM
    end

    subgraph HDFS_Storage
        NN[NameNode - Metadata]
        SNN[Standby NameNode]
        NN <--> SNN
    end

    subgraph Worker_Node_1
        NM1[NodeManager]
        DN1[DataNode]
        AM[ApplicationMaster]
        CON1[Container - Task]
    end

    subgraph Worker_Node_2
        NM2[NodeManager]
        DN2[DataNode]
        CON2[Container - Task]
    end

    C -->|Submit Job| RM
    C -->|Metadata Request| NN
    RM -->|Allocate AM Container| NM1
    NM1 --> AM
    AM -->|Request Resources| RM
    AM -->|Launch Tasks| NM2
    NM2 --> CON2
    DN1 --- DN2
    NN -.->|Block Reports| DN1
    NN -.->|Block Reports| DN2
